item.title: Send Tailscale logs to Azure Blob Storage
item.description: Keep Tailscale logs with the rest of your security data.

item.title: Aperture: Accelerate AI adoption without the lock-in
item.description: Avoid AI lock-in with Aperture’s flexible, identity-aware AI stack

item.title: More Tailscale tricks for your jailbroken Kindle
item.description: Tailscale on jailbroken Kindles now supports proxies and SSH. And a plugin supports Kobos and Pocket Readers, too.

item.title: Redundancy only matters if you can reach it
item.description: HP is integrating Tailscale into Remote System Controller, giving administrators secure access to out-of-band management devices through their existing tailnet.

item.title: Fixing my ridiculous fridge with a tiny Funnel site
item.description: I built a tiny transit dashboard for my fridge. Tailscale Funnel made it easy to share—and reminded me what belongs on the public internet.

item.title: Canada’s Bill C-22 and the security cost of collecting more data
item.description: Bill C-22 would push secure services to collect more data, retain more metadata, and build new access paths. That’s bad for privacy, and bad for security.

item.title: Introducing: Aperture CLI
item.description: Aperture CLI makes AI coding-agent experiments easier.

item.title: Five TailscaleUp sessions I’d attend if I didn’t work here
item.description: I work at Tailscale, so I’m not neutral. But these are the five TailscaleUp sessions I’d still choose first.

item.title: Bambuddy: self-hosted 3D printing beyond the vendor cloud
item.description: Bambuddy helps 3D printer owners keep control local, while Tailscale makes that setup reachable from anywhere.

item.title: Fixing Headlamp OIDC login with Tailscale and tsidp
item.description: Use Tailscale identity to log in to Headlamp and reach Kubernetes RBAC.

item.title: How Cleric uses tsnet to securely automate software operations
item.description: By leveraging Tailscale and the tsnet library, Cleric built a secure connectivity layer that was easy for both customers and Cleric's operations.

item.title: Tailscale + Paperless-ngx: scan everything, expose nothing
item.description: Set up Paperless-ngx with Tailscale to store and securely access your tax, medical, and other documents, with optional AI tagging.

item.title: This month at Tailscale for April 2026
item.description: What's new in Tailscale clients this month, including Aperture features, Kubernetes improvements, and API-only tailnet access.

item.title: Aperture beta: better controls for the AI agent era
item.description: Now available on all plans, Aperture offers better controls and visibility—just in time for the end of subsidized AI.

item.title: Meet tailscale-rs, our new Rust library preview
item.description: An early preview of tailscale-rs is out now, and we’d like your help testing what comes next.
